heading: Clean vehicle credit
label: 30D Clean vehicle credit
notes: References in TextThe Clean Air Act, referred to in subsecs. (d)(1)(D), (3) and (f)(7)(A), is , , which is classified generally to chapter 85 (§ 7401 et seq.) of Title 42, The Public Health and Welfare. Title II of the Act, known as the National Emissions Standards Act, is classified generally to subchapter II (§ 7521 et seq.) of chapter 85 of Title 42. Section 209(b) of the Act is classified to . For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title note set out under and Tables.act July 14, 1955, ch. 36069 Stat. 322section 7543(b) of Title 42section 7401 of Title 42

Effective Date of 2022 Amendment; Transition Rule, , , provided that:Pub. L. 117–169, title I, § 13401(k)136 Stat. 1961Aug. 16, 2022“(1).—In generalExcept as provided in paragraphs (2), (3), (4), and (5), the amendments made by this section [amending this section and sections 30B, 38, 6213, and 6501 of this title] shall apply to vehicles placed in service after .December 31, 2022“(2).—Final assemblyThe amendments made by subsection (b) [amending this section] shall apply to vehicles sold after the date of enactment of this Act [].Aug. 16, 2022“(3).—Per vehicle dollar limitation and related requirementsThe amendments made by subsections (a) and (e) [amending this section] shall apply to vehicles placed in service after the date on which the proposed guidance described in paragraph (3)(B) of section 30D(e) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 (as added by subsection (e)) is issued by the Secretary of the Treasury (or the Secretary’s delegate) [proposed guidance issued , see 88 F.R. 23370].Apr. 17, 2023“(4).—Transfer of creditThe amendments made by subsection (g) [amending this section] shall apply to vehicles placed in service after .December 31, 2023“(5).—Elimination of manufacturer limitationThe amendment made by subsection (d) [amending this section] shall apply to vehicles sold after .”December 31, 2022(), , , provided that:Pub. L. 117–169, title I, § 13401136 Stat. 1962lAug. 16, 2022“Solely for purposes of the application of section 30D of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, in the case of a taxpayer that—“(1)after , and before the date of enactment of this Act [], purchased, or entered into a written binding contract to purchase, a new qualified plug-in electric drive motor vehicle (as defined in section 30D(d)(1) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, as in effect on the day before the date of enactment of this Act), andDecember 31, 2021Aug. 16, 2022“(2)placed such vehicle in service on or after the date of enactment of this Act,such taxpayer may elect (at such time, and in such form and manner, as the Secretary of the Treasury, or the Secretary’s delegate, may prescribe) to treat such vehicle as having been placed in service on the day before the date of enactment of this Act.”

L. 117–169, title I, § 13401(j)136 Stat. 1961Aug. 16, 2022“Beginning in fiscal year 2023 and each fiscal year thereafter, the portion of any credit allowed to an eligible entity (as defined in section 30D(g)(2) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986) pursuant to an election made under section 30D(g) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 that is direct spending shall be increased by 6.0445 percent.”

children.heading: Allowance of credit
children.label: a Allowance of credit
children.text: There shall be allowed as a credit against the tax imposed by this chapter for the taxable year an amount equal to the sum of the credit amounts determined under subsection (b) with respect to each new clean vehicle placed in service by the taxpayer during the taxable year.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The amount determined under this subsection with respect to any new clean vehicle is the sum of the amounts determined under paragraphs (2) and (3) with respect to such vehicle.

children.heading: Critical minerals
children.label: 2 Critical minerals
children.text: In the case of a vehicle with respect to which the requirement described in subsection (e)(1)(A) is satisfied, the amount determined under this paragraph is $3,750.

children.heading: Battery components
children.label: 3 Battery components
children.text: In the case of a vehicle with respect to which the requirement described in subsection (e)(2)(A) is satisfied, the amount determined under this paragraph is $3,750.

children.heading: Business credit treated as part of general business credit
children.label: 1 Business credit treated as part of general business credit
children.text: So much of the credit which would be allowed under subsection (a) for any taxable year (determined without regard to this subsection) that is attributable to property of a character subject to an allowance for depreciation shall be treated as a credit listed in section 38(b) for such taxable year (and not allowed under subsection (a)).

children.heading: Personal credit
children.label: 2 Personal credit
children.text: For purposes of this title, the credit allowed under subsection (a) for any taxable year (determined after application of paragraph (1)) shall be treated as a credit allowable under subpart A for such taxable year.

children.label: A
children.text: the original use of which commences with the taxpayer,

children.label: B
children.text: which is acquired for use or lease by the taxpayer and not for resale,

children.label: C
children.text: which is made by a qualified manufacturer,

children.label: D
children.text: which is treated as a motor vehicle for purposes of title II of the Clean Air Act,

children.label: E
children.text: which has a gross vehicle weight rating of less than 14,000 pounds,

children.label: i
children.text: has a capacity of not less than 7 kilowatt hours, and

children.label: ii
children.text: is capable of being recharged from an external source of electricity,

children.label: G
children.text: the final assembly of which occurs within North America, and

children.label: i
children.text: the name and taxpayer identification number of the taxpayer,

children.label: ii
children.text: the vehicle identification number of the vehicle, unless, in accordance with any applicable rules promulgated by the Secretary of Transportation, the vehicle is not assigned such a number,

children.label: iii
children.text: the battery capacity of the vehicle,

children.label: iv
children.text: verification that original use of the vehicle commences with the taxpayer,

children.label: v
children.text: the maximum credit under this section allowable to the taxpayer with respect to the vehicle, and

children.label: vi
children.text: in the case of a taxpayer who makes an election under subsection (g)(1), any amount described in subsection (g)(2)(C) which has been provided to such taxpayer.

children.heading: Motor vehicle
children.label: 2 Motor vehicle
children.text: The term “motor vehicle” means any vehicle which is manufactured primarily for use on public streets, roads, and highways (not including a vehicle operated exclusively on a rail or rails) and which has at least 4 wheels.

children.heading: Qualified manufacturer
children.label: 3 Qualified manufacturer
children.text: The term “qualified manufacturer” means any manufacturer (within the meaning of the regulations prescribed by the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency for purposes of the administration of title II of the Clean Air Act ( et seq.)) which enters into a written agreement with the Secretary under which such manufacturer agrees to make periodic written reports to the Secretary (at such times and in such manner as the Secretary may provide) providing vehicle identification numbers and such other information related to each vehicle manufactured by such manufacturer as the Secretary may require.42 U.S.C. 7521

children.heading: Battery capacity
children.label: 4 Battery capacity
children.text: The term “capacity” means, with respect to any battery, the quantity of electricity which the battery is capable of storing, expressed in kilowatt hours, as measured from a 100 percent state of charge to a 0 percent state of charge.

children.heading: Final assembly
children.label: 5 Final assembly
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1)(G), the term “final assembly” means the process by which a manufacturer produces a new clean vehicle at, or through the use of, a plant, factory, or other place from which the vehicle is delivered to a dealer or importer with all component parts necessary for the mechanical operation of the vehicle included with the vehicle, whether or not the component parts are permanently installed in or on the vehicle.

children.heading: New qualified fuel cell motor vehicle
children.label: 6 New qualified fuel cell motor vehicle
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “new clean vehicle” shall include any new qualified fuel cell motor vehicle (as defined in section 30B(b)(3)) which meets the requirements under subparagraphs (G) and (H) of paragraph (1).

children.label: A
children.text: any vehicle placed in service after , with respect to which any of the applicable critical minerals contained in the battery of such vehicle (as described in subsection (e)(1)(A)) were extracted, processed, or recycled by a foreign entity of concern (as defined in section 40207(a)(5) of the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act ()), orDecember 31, 202442 U.S.C. 18741(a)(5)

children.label: B
children.text: any vehicle placed in service after , with respect to which any of the components contained in the battery of such vehicle (as described in subsection (e)(2)(A)) were manufactured or assembled by a foreign entity of concern (as so defined).December 31, 2023

children.heading: Critical mineral and battery component requirements
children.label: e Critical mineral and battery component requirements
I
children.text: in the United States, or

children.label: II
children.text: in any country with which the United States has a free trade agreement in effect, or

children.label: ii
children.text: recycled in North America,

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of a vehicle placed in service after the date on which the proposed guidance described in paragraph (3)(B) is issued by the Secretary and before , 40 percent,January 1, 2024

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a vehicle placed in service during calendar year 2024, 50 percent,

children.label: iii
children.text: in the case of a vehicle placed in service during calendar year 2025, 60 percent, and

children.label: iv
children.text: in the case of a vehicle placed in service during calendar year 2026, 70 percent.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The requirement described in this subparagraph with respect to a vehicle is that, with respect to the battery from which the electric motor of such vehicle draws electricity, the percentage of the value of the components contained in such battery that were manufactured or assembled in North America is equal to or greater than the applicable percentage (as certified by the qualified manufacturer, in such form or manner as prescribed by the Secretary).

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of a vehicle placed in service after the date on which the proposed guidance described in paragraph (3)(B) is issued by the Secretary and before , 50 percent,January 1, 2024

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a vehicle placed in service during calendar year 2024 or 2025, 60 percent, and

children.label: iii
children.text: in the case of a vehicle placed in service during calendar year 2026, 70 percent.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall issue such regulations or other guidance as the Secretary determines necessary to carry out the purposes of this subsection, including regulations or other guidance which provides for requirements for recordkeeping or information reporting for purposes of administering the requirements of this subsection.

children.heading: Deadline for proposed guidance
children.label: B Deadline for proposed guidance
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary shall issue proposed guidance with respect to the requirements under this subsection.December 31, 2022

children.heading: Basis reduction
children.label: 1 Basis reduction
children.text: For purposes of this subtitle, the basis of any property for which a credit is allowable under subsection (a) shall be reduced by the amount of such credit so allowed (determined without regard to subsection (c)).

children.heading: No double benefit
children.label: 2 No double benefit
children.text: The amount of any deduction or other credit allowable under this chapter for a vehicle for which a credit is allowable under subsection (a) shall be reduced by the amount of credit allowed under such subsection for such vehicle (determined without regard to subsection (c)).

children.heading: Property used outside United States not qualified
children.label: 4 Property used outside United States not qualified
children.text: No credit shall be allowable under subsection (a) with respect to any property referred to in section 50(b)(1).

children.heading: Recapture
children.label: 5 Recapture
children.text: The Secretary shall, by regulations, provide for recapturing the benefit of any credit allowable under subsection (a) with respect to any property which ceases to be property eligible for such credit.

children.heading: Election not to take credit
children.label: 6 Election not to take credit
children.text: No credit shall be allowed under subsection (a) for any vehicle if the taxpayer elects to not have this section apply to such vehicle.

children.heading: Interaction with air quality and motor vehicle safety standards
children.label: 7 Interaction with air quality and motor vehicle safety standards
A
children.text: the applicable provisions of the Clean Air Act for the applicable make and model year of the vehicle (or applicable air quality provisions of State law in the case of a State which has adopted such provision under a waiver under section 209(b) of the Clean Air Act), and

children.label: B
children.text: the motor vehicle safety provisions of sections 30101 through 30169 of title 49, United States Code.

children.heading: One credit per vehicle
children.label: 8 One credit per vehicle
children.text: In the case of any vehicle, the credit described in subsection (a) shall only be allowed once with respect to such vehicle, as determined based upon the vehicle identification number of such vehicle, including any vehicle with respect to which the taxpayer elects the application of subsection (g).

children.heading: VIN requirement
children.label: 9 VIN requirement
children.text: No credit shall be allowed under this section with respect to any vehicle unless the taxpayer includes the vehicle identification number of such vehicle on the return of tax for the taxable year.

children.heading: Limitation based on modified adjusted gross income
children.label: 10 Limitation based on modified adjusted gross income
I
children.text: the modified adjusted gross income of the taxpayer for such taxable year, or

children.label: II
children.text: the modified adjusted gross income of the taxpayer for the preceding taxable year, exceeds

children.label: ii
children.text: the threshold amount.

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of a joint return or a surviving spouse (as defined in section 2(a)), $300,000,

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a head of household (as defined in section 2(b)), $225,000, and

children.label: iii
children.text: in the case of a taxpayer not described in clause (i) or (ii), $150,000.

children.heading: Modified adjusted gross income
children.label: C Modified adjusted gross income
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “modified adjusted gross income” means adjusted gross income increased by any amount excluded from gross income under section 911, 931, or 933.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: No credit shall be allowed under subsection (a) for a vehicle with a manufacturer’s suggested retail price in excess of the applicable limitation.

children.heading: Vans
children.label: i Vans
children.text: In the case of a van, $80,000.

children.heading: Sport utility vehicles
children.label: ii Sport utility vehicles
children.text: In the case of a sport utility vehicle, $80,000.

children.heading: Pickup trucks
children.label: iii Pickup trucks
children.text: In the case of a pickup truck, $80,000.

children.heading: Other
children.label: iv Other
children.text: In the case of any other vehicle, $55,000.

children.heading: Regulations and guidance
children.label: C Regulations and guidance
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the Secretary shall prescribe such regulations or other guidance as the Secretary determines necessary for determining vehicle classifications using criteria similar to that employed by the Environmental Protection Agency and the Department of the Energy to determine size and class of vehicles.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to such regulations or other guidance as the Secretary determines necessary, if the taxpayer who acquires a new clean vehicle elects the application of this subsection with respect to such vehicle, the credit which would (but for this subsection) be allowed to such taxpayer with respect to such vehicle shall be allowed to the eligible entity specified in such election (and not to such taxpayer).

children.label: A
children.text: subject to paragraph (4), registered with the Secretary for purposes of this paragraph, at such time, and in such form and manner, as the Secretary may prescribe,

children.label: i
children.text: the manufacturer’s suggested retail price,

children.label: ii
children.text: the value of the credit allowed and any other incentive available for the purchase of such vehicle, and

children.label: iii
children.text: the amount provided by the dealer to such taxpayer as a condition of the election described in paragraph (1),

children.label: C
children.text: not later than at the time of such sale, made payment to such taxpayer (whether in cash or in the form of a partial payment or down payment for the purchase of such vehicle) in an amount equal to the credit otherwise allowable to such taxpayer, and

children.label: i
children.text: the availability or use of such incentive shall not limit the ability of a taxpayer to make an election described in paragraph (1), and

children.label: ii
children.text: such election shall not limit the value or use of such incentive.

children.heading: Timing
children.label: 3 Timing
children.text: An election described in paragraph (1) shall be made by the taxpayer not later than the date on which the vehicle for which the credit is allowed under subsection (a) is purchased.

children.heading: Revocation of registration
children.label: 4 Revocation of registration
children.text: Upon determination by the Secretary that a dealer has failed to comply with the requirements described in paragraph (2), the Secretary may revoke the registration (as described in subparagraph (A) of such paragraph) of such dealer.

children.label: A
children.text: shall not be includible in the gross income of the taxpayer, and

children.label: B
children.text: with respect to the dealer, shall not be deductible under this title.

children.label: A
children.text: the requirements of paragraphs (1) and (2) of subsection (f) shall apply to the taxpayer who acquired the vehicle in the same manner as if the credit determined under this section with respect to such vehicle were allowed to such taxpayer,

children.label: B
children.text: paragraph (6) of such subsection shall not apply, and

children.label: C
children.text: the requirement of paragraph (9) of such subsection (f) shall be treated as satisfied if the eligible entity provides the vehicle identification number of such vehicle to the Secretary in such manner as the Secretary may provide.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a program to make advance payments to any eligible entity in an amount equal to the cumulative amount of the credits allowed under subsection (a) with respect to any vehicles sold by such entity for which an election described in paragraph (1) has been made.

children.heading: Excessive payments
children.label: B Excessive payments
children.text: Rules similar to the rules of section 6417(d)(6) shall apply for purposes of this paragraph.

children.heading: Treatment of advance payments
children.label: C Treatment of advance payments
children.text: For purposes of , United States Code, the payments under subparagraph (A) shall be treated in the same manner as a refund due from a credit provision referred to in subsection (b)(2) of such section.section 1324 of title 31

children.heading: Dealer
children.label: 8 Dealer
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “dealer” means a person licensed by a State, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, any other territory or possession of the United States, an Indian tribal government, or any Alaska Native Corporation (as defined in section 3 of the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act () to engage in the sale of vehicles.43 U.S.C. 1602(m)1So in original. Another closing parenthesis probably should appear.1

children.heading: Indian tribal government
children.label: 9 Indian tribal government
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “Indian tribal government” means the recognized governing body of any Indian or Alaska Native tribe, band, nation, pueblo, village, community, component band, or component reservation, individually identified (including parenthetically) in the list published most recently as of the date of enactment of this subsection pursuant to section 104 of the Federally Recognized Indian Tribe List Act of 1994 ().25 U.S.C. 5131

children.heading: Recapture
children.label: 10 Recapture
children.text: In the case of any taxpayer who has made an election described in paragraph (1) with respect to a new clean vehicle and received a payment described in paragraph (2)(C) from an eligible entity, if the credit under subsection (a) would otherwise (but for this subsection) not be allowable to such taxpayer pursuant to the application of subsection (f)(10), the tax imposed on such taxpayer under this chapter for the taxable year in which such vehicle was placed in service shall be increased by the amount of the payment received by such taxpayer.

children.heading: Termination
children.label: h Termination
children.text: No credit shall be allowed under this section with respect to any vehicle acquired after .September 30, 2025
